refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥0.97 (97%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the system is available for productive operation to total scheduled operational time, accounting for planned maintenance, unplanned downtime, and safety-driven shutdowns.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts customer satisfaction and mission success. Refinement clarifies that availability is a measurable, time-based metric with explicit exclusions (planned maintenance) and inclusions (safety-driven stops). High safety criticality demands that availability measurement does not mask safety-critical failures; safety shutdowns are counted as unavailability but are necessary. This prevents gaming the metric by hiding safety issues.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous telemetry logging of system state transitions (operational, maintenance, fault, safety-shutdown) with timestamped records. Availability calculated as (Total Scheduled Time − Unplanned Downtime − Safety-Driven Shutdown Duration) / Total Scheduled Time. Monthly and cumulative reporting against 0.97 target.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The system shall detect and log all fault conditions that result in loss of operational capability within 5 seconds.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The system shall provide predictive maintenance alerts to minimize unplanned downtime.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The system shall enable rapid fault diagnosis and recovery procedures to reduce mean time to repair (MTTR).
FR-NGHE-004.4: The system shall maintain safety-critical functions even during partial system failures to prevent unnecessary safety-driven shutdowns.
FR-NGHE-004.5: The system shall provide real-time availability status visibility to Site Manager and Maintenance teams.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement redundant critical subsystems (dual motors, dual control channels, dual sensors) with automatic failover logic to mask single-point failures and maintain availability.
DP-B: Implement comprehensive health monitoring, predictive diagnostics, and rapid fault isolation with centralized availability tracking and reporting dashboard.
DP-C: Implement modular architecture with hot-swappable components and standardized quick-disconnect interfaces to enable rapid component replacement without full system shutdown.
DP-D: Implement combination of redundancy (DP-A), health monitoring (DP-B), and modularity (DP-C) with integrated availability management system that coordinates all three strategies.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Implement integrated availability management system combining (1) redundant critical subsystems with automatic failover, (2) comprehensive health monitoring with predictive diagnostics and centralized availability tracking, and (3) modular architecture with hot-swappable components and quick-disconnect interfaces. Availability is managed through a coordinated strategy: redundancy masks transient faults, health monitoring enables predictive maintenance to prevent failures, and modularity enables rapid repair without full shutdown.

potential_sources: Redundancy strategy (DP-A) is independent of health monitoring (DP-B); they operate in parallel without functional coupling.
Modularity (DP-C) is orthogonal to both redundancy and monitoring; it affects physical/software packaging but not functional logic.

Integration point: Availability management system (DP-D) must coordinate failover decisions (from DP-A) with health data (from DP-B) and maintenance scheduling (from DP-C). This is a control/coordination coupling, not a functional decomposition coupling. Manageable via clear interface contracts.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is a composite strategy rather than a single mechanism. Each sub-strategy (redundancy, monitoring, modularity) can be designed independently and integrated via well-defined availability management interfaces. No hidden functional dependencies detected.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Redundant subsystems can be implemented cost-effectively within project budget and weight constraints for heavy equipment.
ASSUMPTION: Predictive diagnostics algorithms can achieve sufficient accuracy (>90% true positive rate) to justify maintenance actions.
ASSUMPTION: Hot-swappable components can be designed without compromising structural integrity or safety margins.
RISK: Redundancy increases system complexity and potential for mode confusion (operator unsure which subsystem is active). Mitigation: Clear status indication and automated failover logic.
RISK: Health monitoring generates false positives, leading to unnecessary maintenance actions and reduced availability. Mitigation: Validation of diagnostic algorithms against field data.

RISK: Modularity may introduce additional failure modes at quick-disconnect interfaces (e.g., incomplete connections, contamination). Mitigation: Design interfaces with positive locking and environmental sealing.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-D is sound but requires explicit sub-DPs for each strategy (redundancy, monitoring, modularity) to be decomposed at the next level. Clarify that 'availability management system' is a logical coordination layer, not a separate physical component. Define clear success criteria for each sub-strategy (e.g., redundancy: <5% performance degradation on failover; monitoring: <2% false positive rate; modularity: <10 min component swap time).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and provides the unified availability management system. This module owns the availability KPI, coordinates redundancy strategies across subsystems, aggregates health monitoring data, and provides availability status reporting to stakeholders.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution outputs continuous telemetry including system state (operational/fault/maintenance/safety-shutdown), subsystem health metrics, availability status, and diagnostic data to external monitoring systems and stakeholders.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives operational tasking (mission commands, maintenance schedules, availability targets) from Site Manager and Tele-operator via Tasking interface.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution outputs safety-critical alerts and shutdown commands via SafetyAlert interface to Safety Services and external safety systems.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution communicates with remote monitoring, predictive maintenance systems, and cloud-based analytics via Network Link for health data upload and diagnostic algorithm updates.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of the availability KPI. It integrates Vehicle (hardware availability), Teleoperation (remote operation availability), and Safety Services (safety-driven shutdown logic). Availability management system logic (redundancy coordination, health aggregation, status reporting) is naturally contained at this level. Minor diffusion: some redundancy logic may reside in Vehicle subsystem (e.g., motor failover), but coordination is at NGHE Solution level.

Vehicle owns physical redundancy (dual motors, dual sensors) and local health monitoring. However, availability KPI is a system-level metric that requires coordination across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Assigning to Vehicle alone would fragment availability management and create diffusion of responsibility for the KPI.

Teleoperation contributes to availability (remote operation reduces downtime for certain tasks) but does not own the availability KPI. Teleoperation is a mode of operation, not the primary availability mechanism. High diffusion if assigned here.

Safety Services owns safety-driven shutdown logic, which affects availability metrics. However, Safety Services is a constraint on availability (safety shutdowns reduce measured availability), not the primary mechanism for achieving the KPI. Assigning availability KPI to Safety Services would create a conflict of interest (safety vs. availability optimization).

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the correct owner of CR-NGHE-004 (Availability KPI) because: (1) Availability is a system-level emergent property that depends on the coordinated behavior of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. (2) The availability management system (redundancy coordination, health aggregation, status reporting) is a cross-cutting concern that must reside at the system integration level. (3) NGHE Solution has visibility into all subsystem states and can make holistic availability optimization decisions. (4) Ownership at NGHE Solution level prevents diffusion of responsibility and enables clear accountability for the KPI.
containment_risks: RISK: If redundancy logic is distributed across Vehicle and NGHE Solution, coordination failures may occur (e.g., both subsystems attempt failover simultaneously). Mitigation: Define clear ownership of failover decision logic (recommend: NGHE Solution owns coordination, Vehicle owns local redundancy implementation).

RISK: Health monitoring data from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services must be aggregated at NGHE Solution level. If data formats or update rates are inconsistent, availability calculations may be inaccurate. Mitigation: Define standardized health data schema and synchronization protocol.

RISK: Availability status reporting to stakeholders (Site Manager, Maintenance) must be timely and accurate. If NGHE Solution does not have real-time visibility into all subsystem states, reporting delays may mask failures. Mitigation: Implement continuous telemetry with <1 second latency for critical state changes.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary. Recommend explicit sub-modules within NGHE Solution for (1) Redundancy Coordinator (owns failover logic), (2) Health Monitor (owns data aggregation and diagnostics), and (3) Availability Reporter (owns KPI calculation and stakeholder reporting). These sub-modules should be decomposed at the next level. Ensure clear interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services for health data, failover commands, and status updates.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (integrated availability management system) directly addresses FR (achieve ≥0.97 availability) through three complementary mechanisms: (1) Redundancy masks transient faults and reduces unplanned downtime. (2) Health monitoring enables predictive maintenance to prevent failures. (3) Modularity enables rapid repair without full shutdown. The DP is complete, feasible, and measurable. No functional gaps detected.

diffusion_risks: Redundancy logic may be partially implemented in Vehicle subsystem (e.g., motor failover) and partially in NGHE Solution (e.g., failover coordination). Clear ownership boundaries must be defined to prevent diffusion.

Health monitoring data flows from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to NGHE Solution. If data collection is inconsistent across subsystems, availability calculations may be inaccurate. Standardized telemetry schema required.
Modularity (hot-swappable components) is primarily a Vehicle concern, but NGHE Solution must track component status and maintenance schedules. Interface contract required.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary for the availability KPI. It owns the availability management system (coordination, aggregation, reporting) and integrates the three sub-strategies (redundancy, monitoring, modularity) implemented across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Containment score 0.95 is acceptable; minor diffusion (0.05) is expected at system integration level.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Availability KPI is directly measurable via continuous telemetry logging and timestamped state transitions. Verification is feasible: collect operational data over agreed evaluation period, calculate availability ratio, compare against 0.97 target. Acceptance criteria are clear and objective.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Availability KPI is allocated to NGHE Solution, which owns the availability management system. Sub-FRs (fault detection, predictive maintenance, rapid diagnosis, safety-critical redundancy, status visibility) are allocated to appropriate subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) at the next decomposition level. Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit sub-DPs for redundancy, health monitoring, and modularity strategies. Each sub-DP should have measurable success criteria (e.g., redundancy: <5% performance degradation; monitoring: <2% false positive rate; modularity: <10 min swap time).
Establish clear ownership boundaries between NGHE Solution and Vehicle for redundancy logic. Recommend: NGHE Solution owns failover coordination, Vehicle owns local redundancy implementation.
Define standardized health data schema and telemetry protocol for data flow from Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services to NGHE Solution. Ensure <1 second latency for critical state changes.
Develop availability calculation algorithm with explicit handling of planned maintenance, unplanned downtime, and safety-driven shutdowns. Validate algorithm against historical field data.
Plan for validation of predictive diagnostics algorithms (target: >90% true positive rate) before deployment. Establish feedback loop for algorithm refinement based on field data.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-004 (Availability KPI) is a system-level requirement that is not atomic and requires decomposition into sub-FRs. The refined FR identifies five sub-FRs (fault detection, predictive maintenance, rapid diagnosis, safety-critical redundancy, status visibility) that must be decomposed further. DP-D (integrated availability management system) is sound and passes FR-DP coupling check. IM assignment to NGHE Solution is correct and passes DP-IM gate. However, the sub-FRs must be decomposed to the next level to allocate responsibility to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Proceed with decomposition of sub-FRs.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The system shall detect and log all fault conditions that result in loss of operational capability within 5 seconds.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The system shall provide predictive maintenance alerts to minimize unplanned downtime.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The system shall enable rapid fault diagnosis and recovery procedures to reduce mean time to repair (MTTR).
FR-NGHE-004.4: The system shall maintain safety-critical functions even during partial system failures to prevent unnecessary safety-driven shutdowns.
FR-NGHE-004.5: The system shall provide real-time availability status visibility to Site Manager and Maintenance teams.

assumptions: Redundant subsystems can be implemented cost-effectively within project budget and weight constraints.
Predictive diagnostics algorithms can achieve >90% true positive rate.
Hot-swappable components can be designed without compromising safety margins.
Availability is measured as (Total Scheduled Time − Unplanned Downtime − Safety-Driven Shutdown Duration) / Total Scheduled Time.
Safety-driven shutdowns are counted as unavailability but are necessary and acceptable.
Telemetry data can be collected and aggregated with <1 second latency for critical state changes.
open_risks: Redundancy increases system complexity and potential for mode confusion. Mitigation: Clear status indication and automated failover logic.
Health monitoring generates false positives, leading to unnecessary maintenance. Mitigation: Validation of diagnostic algorithms against field data.
Modularity may introduce failure modes at quick-disconnect interfaces. Mitigation: Design interfaces with positive locking and environmental sealing.
Redundancy logic may be distributed across Vehicle and NGHE Solution, causing coordination failures. Mitigation: Define clear ownership of failover decision logic.
Health monitoring data formats may be inconsistent across subsystems. Mitigation: Define standardized health data schema.
Availability status reporting may have delays if NGHE Solution lacks real-time visibility. Mitigation: Implement continuous telemetry with <1 second latency.
evidence_needed: Validation of predictive diagnostics algorithms against historical field data (target: >90% true positive rate).
Design review of redundancy logic and failover coordination to ensure no mode confusion.
Design review of quick-disconnect interfaces to ensure positive locking and environmental sealing.
Specification of standardized health data schema and telemetry protocol.
Specification of availability calculation algorithm with explicit handling of planned maintenance, unplanned downtime, and safety-driven shutdowns.
Field trial data demonstrating achievement of ≥0.97 availability over agreed evaluation period.
